title: CASE/UCO SDK Extension Manifest
description: Metadata schema for extension ontologies that extend the CASE/UCO ecosystem. Each extension directory under extensions/ must contain a manifest.json conforming to this schema.

name.description: Short identifier used as the extension directory name and package suffix (e.g., 'cac', 'aeo').

display_name.description: Human-readable name for documentation and CLI output.

version.description: Version of the upstream ontology this extension tracks. Extension binding packages use this as their package version.

upstream_repo.description: URL of the upstream ontology repository.

upstream_ref.description: Git tag, branch, or commit SHA the submodule is pinned to.

license.description: SPDX license identifier for the extension ontology.

namespaces.description: Map of prefix -> namespace URI for all namespaces defined by this extension. Used by the generator for module classification instead of heuristic detection.

ontology_submodule.description: Relative path from the extension directory to the git submodule root (typically 'ontology').

owl_files.description: Relative paths (from extension directory) to OWL definition files to parse. Order matters: load spine/core files before domain modules.

shacl_files.description: Relative paths to SHACL shapes files. May be empty if SHACL is embedded in OWL files (as with AEO).

bridge_files.description: Relative paths to bridge/alignment modules (e.g., UCO bridge, CASE bridge, gUFO bridge). Loaded for validation but not for class generation.

exemplar_files.description: Relative paths to example instance data files (A-Box). Used for validation testing only.

uco_compat.description: List of UCO/CASE versions this extension is known to be compatible with (e.g., ['1.4.0', '1.5.0']).

upper_ontology.description: Which upper/foundational ontology this extension directly depends on, if any.

cdo_shapes_compatibility.description: Compatibility status of each CDO Shapes profile with this extension. 'included' means the extension already provides this alignment. 'not_recommended' means loading both may produce conflicting axioms.
